# Swirski UI Full LLM Context

Generated from `packages/ui/package.json`, `registry/swirski.registry.json`, and docs metadata in `apps/docs/content`.

Current package version: `@swirski/ui@1.2.9`.

Docs site: https://ui.swirski.dev

## What Swirski UI Is

Swirski UI is a React component package for bold, graphic, studio-flavored interfaces. It combines thick borders, hard shadows, expressive typography, custom cursors, practical app primitives, theme tokens, docs, playgrounds, generated props metadata, examples, and registry workflows.

Use `@swirski/ui` for normal package installs, `@swirski/cli` for source-copy ownership, and hosted shadcn-compatible registry items for shadcn workflows.

The visual language is loud but practical: strong borders, hard offset shadows, graphic color, uppercase labels, expressive display fonts, dense app primitives, and `className` escape hatches.

## Install Workflows

### Package

```bash
pnpm add @swirski/ui
```

Import styles once in the app entry:

```tsx
import "@swirski/ui/styles.css";
```

Import components from the package root:

```tsx
import { Button, Card, CardContent, Text, Title } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Tailwind CSS v4 package consumers should make Tailwind scan the package output:

```css
@source "../node_modules/@swirski/ui/dist";
```

### Swirski CLI

```bash
pnpm add -D @swirski/cli
pnpm exec swirski init
pnpm exec swirski add button
pnpm exec swirski add button card dialog
```

### shadcn Registry

```bash
pnpm dlx shadcn@latest view https://ui.swirski.dev/r/button.json
pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://ui.swirski.dev/r/button.json
```

```json
{
  "registries": {
    "@swirski": "https://ui.swirski.dev/r/{name}.json"
  }
}
```

Registry root: https://ui.swirski.dev/registry.json

## Theme Provider

```tsx
import { SwirskiProvider, createSwirskiTheme } from "@swirski/ui";

const theme = createSwirskiTheme({
  colorBlue: "#0047FF",
  colorYellow: "#FFE45C",
  shadowMd: "8px 8px 0 var(--sw-color-shadow)",
});

export function App() {
  return (
    <SwirskiProvider theme={theme}>
      <YourRoutes />
    </SwirskiProvider>
  );
}
```

Core tokens:

```txt
--sw-color-ink
--sw-color-paper
--sw-color-surface
--sw-color-muted
--sw-color-blue
--sw-color-yellow
--sw-color-red
--sw-color-shadow
--sw-color-focus
--sw-border-width
--sw-shadow-sm
--sw-shadow-md
--sw-shadow-lg
--sw-font-body
--sw-font-heading
--sw-font-display
```

## Code Generation Guidance

- Import package examples from `@swirski/ui`.
- Use copied-source imports only when the user is using `@swirski/cli` or shadcn registry installs.
- Prefer `variant`, `size`, `tone`, native props, forwarded refs, and `className` escape hatches.
- Prefer theme tokens and shared system helpers over hardcoded one-off styles.
- Preserve native HTML semantics where Swirski already uses them.
- Use `@floating-ui/dom` positioning through the shared system helper for floating content.
- Keep docs metadata, registry metadata, and package exports in sync when adding components.

## Backgrounds

### DiagonalLines

A configurable diagonal stripe background for punchy poster and pop-art compositions.

Category: Backgrounds
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/diagonal-lines
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/diagonal-lines.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/diagonal-lines`

Import:
```tsx
import { DiagonalLines } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<div className="relative h-64 overflow-hidden bg-[#FFD400]">
  <DiagonalLines
    className="inset-0"
    angle={-35}
    color="#0B0B0C"
    opacity={0.3}
    spacing={18}
    thickness={2}
    accentColor="#FF3131"
    accentEvery={6}
    accentThickness={8}
  />
</div>
```

Props:
- `angle`: `number | string` default -45 - Line angle in degrees or any CSS angle value.
- `color`: `string` default "#0B0B0C" - Primary stripe color.
- `opacity`: `CSSProperties['opacity']` default 0.2 - Opacity applied to the stripe layer.
- `spacing`: `number | string` default 18 - Distance before the pattern repeats.
- `thickness`: `number | string` default 2 - Primary stripe thickness.
- `accentColor`: `string` - Optional color for heavier accent stripes.
- `accentEvery`: `number` - Adds accent stripes every N repeats when greater than 1.
- `accentThickness`: `number | string` default 5 - Thickness for accent stripes.
- `...divProps`: `Omit<HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "color">` - Forwarded to the root div.

### DotGrid

A configurable dot-grid background for subtle texture, halftone fields and pop-art surfaces.

Category: Backgrounds
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/dot-grid
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/dot-grid.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/dot-grid`

Import:
```tsx
import { DotGrid } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<div className="relative h-64 overflow-hidden bg-[#FFD400]">
  <DotGrid
    className="inset-0"
    color="#0B0B0C"
    opacity={0.38}
    spacing={14}
    dotSize={1.8}
    accentColor="#FF3131"
    accentEvery={5}
    accentDotSize={5}
  />
</div>
```

Props:
- `color`: `string` default "currentColor" - Primary dot color.
- `opacity`: `CSSProperties['opacity']` - Opacity applied to the grid layer.
- `spacing`: `number | string` default 13 - Distance between dots.
- `dotSize`: `number | string` default 1.2 - Primary dot radius.
- `accentColor`: `string` - Optional color for larger accent dots.
- `accentEvery`: `number` - Adds an accent dot every N grid cells when greater than 1.
- `accentDotSize`: `number | string` default 3 - Accent dot radius.
- `...divProps`: `Omit<HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "color">` - Forwarded to the root div.

### LineGrid

A configurable line grid background with optional heavier accent lines for pop-art surfaces.

Category: Backgrounds
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/line-grid
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/line-grid.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/line-grid`

Import:
```tsx
import { LineGrid } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<div className="relative h-64 overflow-hidden bg-[#FFD400]">
  <LineGrid
    className="inset-0"
    horizontalColor="#0B0B0C"
    horizontalSpacing={18}
    horizontalThickness={2}
    verticalColor="#0057FF"
    verticalSpacing={26}
    verticalThickness={1}
    opacity={0.34}
    accentColor="#FF3131"
    accentEvery={4}
    accentThickness={5}
  />
</div>
```

Props:
- `color`: `string` default "#0B0B0C" - Fallback color for horizontal and vertical lines.
- `opacity`: `CSSProperties['opacity']` default 0.2 - Opacity applied to the grid layer.
- `spacing`: `number | string` default 18 - Fallback spacing for both directions.
- `thickness`: `number | string` default 1 - Fallback line thickness for both directions.
- `direction`: `"both" | "horizontal" | "vertical"` default "both" - Which line directions to render.
- `horizontalColor`: `string` - Overrides the horizontal line color.
- `verticalColor`: `string` - Overrides the vertical line color.
- `accentColor`: `string` - Optional color for heavier accent lines.
- `accentEvery`: `number` - Adds accent lines every N cells when greater than 1.
- `accentThickness`: `number | string` default 3 - Thickness for accent lines.
- `...divProps`: `Omit<HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "color">` - Forwarded to the root div.

## Buttons

### Button

A bold Swirski UI button used for links, actions and call-to-actions.

Category: Buttons
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/button
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/button.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/button`

Import:
```tsx
import { Button } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Button href="/pieces">View pieces</Button>
```

Props:
- `children`: `ReactNode` required - Button label or inline content.
- `href`: `string` - Renders the button as an anchor when provided.
- `as`: `ElementType` - Custom component used for the root element, such as Next.js Link.
- `tone`: `"blue" | "yellow" | "red" | "white" | "black"` default "blue" - Applies the Swirski color treatment.
- `variant`: `"solid" | "outline" | "ghost"` default "solid" - Controls the visual treatment independently from tone.
- `size`: `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` default "md" - Controls the button scale.
- `asChild`: `boolean` default false - Passes button props to a single child element.
- `icon`: `"arrow-up-right" | "github"` - Adds a bundled icon beside the label.
- `iconSide`: `"left" | "right"` default "left" - Controls where the optional icon is rendered.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the root button or anchor.
- `...anchorProps`: `AnchorHTMLAttributes<HTMLAnchorElement>` - Forwarded when the component renders an anchor.
- `...buttonProps`: `ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>` - Forwarded when the component renders a button.

## Cards

### Card

A bordered content block for projects, pieces, links and previews.

Category: Cards
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/card
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/card.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/card`

Import:
```tsx
import { Card, CardContent, CardTitle, Text } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Card>
  <CardContent>
    <CardTitle>Patchwork Jacket</CardTitle>
    <Text className="mt-3" tone="muted" weight="bold">
      A framed content primitive with Swirski borders and shadows.
    </Text>
  </CardContent>
</Card>
```

Composition:
```txt
Card
`-- CardContent
    |-- CardTitle
    `-- children
```

Props:
- `children`: `ReactNode` required - Card body content.
- `interactive`: `boolean` default true - Enables hover and active movement styles.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the card root.
- `CardContent.className`: `string` - Adds classes to the padded content wrapper.
- `CardTitle.children`: `ReactNode` required - Title content rendered inside the card heading.

## Disclosure

### Accordion

A disclosure component built on details and summary, with no client JavaScript required.

Category: Disclosure
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/accordion
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/accordion.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/accordion`

Import:
```tsx
import {
  Accordion,
  AccordionContent,
  AccordionItem,
  AccordionTrigger,
} from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Accordion>
  <AccordionItem open>
    <AccordionTrigger>What makes it Swirski?</AccordionTrigger>
    <AccordionContent>
      Thick borders, punchy color, loud type and plain HTML behavior.
    </AccordionContent>
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>
```

Composition:
```txt
Accordion
`-- AccordionItem
    |-- AccordionTrigger
    `-- AccordionContent
```

Props:
- `Accordion.className`: `string` - Adds classes to the accordion wrapper.
- `AccordionItem.open`: `boolean` default false - Uses the native details open state.
- `AccordionItem.className`: `string` - Adds classes to the details element.
- `AccordionTrigger.children`: `ReactNode` required - Summary label for the disclosure item.
- `AccordionTrigger.className`: `string` - Adds classes to the summary element.
- `AccordionContent.className`: `string` - Adds classes to the content panel.
- `...detailsProps`: `DetailsHTMLAttributes<HTMLDetailsElement>` - Forwarded to each accordion item.

## Feedback

### Alert

A loud composable feedback block for announcements, errors and status messages.

Category: Feedback
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/alert
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/alert.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/alert`

Import:
```tsx
import { Alert, AlertTitle, AlertDescription } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Alert tone="yellow">
  <AlertTitle>Heads up</AlertTitle>
  <AlertDescription>New components are ready to try.</AlertDescription>
</Alert>
```

Props:
- `children`: `ReactNode` required - Alert content, usually AlertTitle and AlertDescription.
- `tone`: `"blue" | "yellow" | "red" | "white"` default "yellow" - Applies the alert surface color.
- `role`: `AriaRole` default "status" - Accessible live-region role for the alert container.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the root alert.
- `AlertTitle.className`: `string` - Adds classes to the title heading.
- `AlertDescription.className`: `string` - Adds classes to the description paragraph.

### Badge

A compact high-contrast label for statuses, tags and small interface signals.

Category: Feedback
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/badge
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/badge.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/badge`

Import:
```tsx
import { Badge } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Badge tone="yellow">New drop</Badge>
```

Props:
- `children`: `ReactNode` required - Badge text or inline content.
- `tone`: `"blue" | "yellow" | "red" | "white" | "black"` default "yellow" - Applies one of the Swirski status color treatments.
- `size`: `"sm" | "md"` default "md" - Controls the compact badge padding and text size.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the root span.
- `...spanProps`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>` - Forwarded to the root span.

### Loader

A compact loading spinner for pending states.

Category: Feedback
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/loader
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/loader.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/loader`

Import:
```tsx
import { Loader } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Loader size="md" />
```

Props:
- `size`: `"sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" | "2xl"` default "md" - Controls the spinner size.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the spinner.

### Progress

A simple progress meter for loading and completion states.

Category: Feedback
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/progress
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/progress.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/progress`

Import:
```tsx
import { Progress } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Progress value={64} />
```

Props:
- `value`: `number` default 0 - Current progress value.
- `max`: `number` default 100 - Maximum progress value.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the progress track.

### Skeleton

A placeholder block for loading layouts.

Category: Feedback
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/skeleton
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/skeleton.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/skeleton`

Import:
```tsx
import { Skeleton } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Skeleton className="h-24 w-full" />
```

Props:
- `className`: `string` - Controls the skeleton size and extra styling.
- `...divProps`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>` - Forwarded to the root div.

### Toast

Status messages for save states, confirmations and errors.

Category: Feedback
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/toast
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/toast.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/toast`
Copy dependencies: `use-portal-root`

Import:
```tsx
import { Toast, ToastDescription, ToastTitle } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Toast tone="yellow">
  <ToastTitle>Saved</ToastTitle>
  <ToastDescription>Your changes are live.</ToastDescription>
</Toast>
```

Props:
- `tone`: `"blue" | "yellow" | "red" | "white"` default "yellow" - Applies the toast color treatment.
- `ToastProvider`: `{ children: ReactNode }` - Provider for managed toasts and useToast.
- `useToast`: `() => ToastContextValue` - Adds and removes managed toasts.

## Forms

### Checkbox

A native checkbox with a chunky Swirski mark, label and optional description.

Category: Forms
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/checkbox
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/checkbox.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/checkbox`

Import:
```tsx
import { Checkbox } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Checkbox
  label="Send launch updates"
  description="A compact, native checkbox with a custom Swirski mark."
/>
```

Props:
- `label`: `ReactNode` - Visible label rendered beside the checkbox.
- `description`: `ReactNode` - Optional supporting text below the label.
- `containerClassName`: `string` - Adds classes to the root label wrapper.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the hidden native checkbox input.
- `disabled`: `boolean` default false - Disables interaction and dims the wrapper.
- `...inputProps`: `Omit<InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "type">` - Forwarded to the native checkbox input.

### Field

A form field set with label, input, textarea and hint primitives built from native elements.

Category: Forms
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/field
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/field.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/field`

Import:
```tsx
import {
  Field,
  FieldError,
  FieldHint,
  Input,
  Label,
  Textarea,
} from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Field>
  <Label htmlFor="email">Email</Label>
  <Input id="email" placeholder="studio@swirski.dev" />
  <FieldHint>Use the address where clients can reach you.</FieldHint>
</Field>
```

Composition:
```txt
Field
|-- Label
|-- Input | Textarea | Select | Checkbox | RadioGroup
|-- FieldHint
`-- FieldError
```

Props:
- `Field.className`: `string` - Adds classes to the field wrapper.
- `Label.htmlFor`: `string` - Connects the label to an input or textarea id.
- `Label.className`: `string` - Adds classes to the label.
- `Input`: `InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>` - Forwards native input props and refs.
- `Textarea`: `TextareaHTMLAttributes<HTMLTextAreaElement>` - Forwards native textarea props and refs.
- `FieldHint.className`: `string` - Adds classes to helper text.
- `FieldError.className`: `string` - Adds classes to validation text.

### RadioGroup

A native radio group for choosing one option from a set.

Category: Forms
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/radio-group
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/radio-group.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/radio-group`

Import:
```tsx
import { RadioGroup } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<RadioGroup
  name="tone"
  defaultValue="blue"
  options={[
    { value: "blue", label: "Blue" },
    { value: "yellow", label: "Yellow" },
  ]}
/>
```

Props:
- `name`: `string` required - Native radio group name.
- `options`: `RadioGroupOption[]` required - Options rendered as radio inputs.
- `value`: `string` - Controlled selected value.
- `defaultValue`: `string` - Initial selected value.
- `onValueChange`: `(value: string) => void` - Called when the selected value changes.

### Select

A custom dropdown picker for option sets, playground controls and compact forms.

Category: Forms
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/select
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/select.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/select`
Copy dependencies: `use-portal-root`

Import:
```tsx
import { Select } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Select
  placeholder="Pick a tone"
  options={[
    { value: "yellow", label: "Yellow" },
    { value: "blue", label: "Blue" },
    { value: "red", label: "Red" },
  ]}
/>
```

Composition:
```txt
Field
|-- Label
|-- Select
|   `-- options[]
|       |-- value
|       `-- label
`-- FieldHint | FieldError
```

Props:
- `options`: `SelectOption[]` required - Options shown in the dropdown list.
- `value`: `string` - Controlled selected option value.
- `defaultValue`: `string` - Initial selected option value when uncontrolled.
- `onValueChange`: `(value: string) => void` - Called when a new option is selected.
- `placeholder`: `string` default "Select an option" - Text shown when no option is selected.
- `disabled`: `boolean` default false - Disables the trigger and prevents selection.
- `name`: `string` - Adds a hidden input for native form submission.
- `triggerClassName`: `string` - Adds classes to the dropdown trigger.
- `contentClassName`: `string` - Adds classes to the dropdown content panel.
- `optionClassName`: `string` - Adds classes to each dropdown option.

### Slider

A native range input for numeric controls.

Category: Forms
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/slider
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/slider.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/slider`

Import:
```tsx
import { Slider } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Slider defaultValue={64} min={0} max={100} />
```

Props:
- `...inputProps`: `InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>` - Forwarded to the native range input.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the range input.

### Switch

A native checkbox presented as a switch for binary preferences and settings.

Category: Forms
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/switch
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/switch.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/switch`

Import:
```tsx
import { Switch } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Switch label="Studio mode" defaultChecked />
```

Props:
- `label`: `ReactNode` - Visible label rendered beside the switch.
- `description`: `ReactNode` - Optional supporting text below the label.
- `containerClassName`: `string` - Adds classes to the root label wrapper.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the hidden native checkbox input.
- `disabled`: `boolean` default false - Disables interaction and dims the wrapper.
- `...inputProps`: `Omit<InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>, "type">` - Forwarded to the native switch input.

## Interaction

### Cursor

A playful pixel-art cursor provider with arrow, hover, click, text, zoom, panning and status states.

Category: Interaction
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/cursor
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/cursor.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/cursor`

Import:
```tsx
import { Button, CursorProvider, CursorDock } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<CursorProvider>
  <CursorDock />
  <Button href="/components">Hover me</Button>
  <div data-cursor="pan">Pan surface</div>
</CursorProvider>
```

Props:
- `CursorProvider.children`: `ReactNode` required - Content that should receive the Swirski cursor styles.
- `CursorProvider.cursor`: `CursorId` - Controlled cursor id.
- `CursorProvider.defaultCursor`: `CursorId` default "bolt" - Initial cursor when the provider is uncontrolled.
- `CursorProvider.cursors`: `SwirskiCursor[]` - Custom cursor sets. Optional fields can supply pointer, active, disabled, text, zoom, grab, grabbing, busy and status cursor assets.
- `CursorProvider.storageKey`: `string | false` default "swirski-cursor" - Local storage key, or false to skip persistence.
- `data-cursor`: `"pointer" | "active" | "disabled" | "text" | "zoom-in" | "zoom-out" | "pan" | "grab" | "grabbing" | "move" | "resize" | "copy" | "crosshair" | "help" | "progress" | "wait" | "not-allowed"` - Opts any descendant into a specific cursor state. Tailwind cursor utility classes are recognized too.
- `CursorDock.position`: `"fixed" | "absolute"` default "fixed" - Positions the dock against the viewport or parent.
- `CursorDock.side`: `"left" | "right"` default "right" - Side where the cursor dock opens.
- `CursorDock.label`: `string` default "Choose cursor" - Accessible label for the dock trigger.

### Dialog

A modal overlay for confirmations, details and focused forms.

Category: Interaction
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/dialog
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/dialog.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/dialog`
Copy dependencies: `use-portal-root`

Import:
```tsx
import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogTrigger } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Dialog>
  <DialogTrigger>Open dialog</DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Delete piece?</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>This action needs confirmation.</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    <DialogFooter>
      <DialogClose>Cancel</DialogClose>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>
```

Composition:
```txt
Dialog
|-- DialogTrigger
`-- DialogContent
    |-- DialogHeader
    |   |-- DialogTitle
    |   `-- DialogDescription
    |-- DialogFooter
    `-- DialogClose
```

Props:
- `open`: `boolean` - Controlled open state.
- `defaultOpen`: `boolean` default false - Initial uncontrolled open state.
- `onOpenChange`: `(open: boolean) => void` - Called when the dialog opens or closes.
- `className`: `string` - Available on trigger, content and layout slots.

### Drawer

A side sheet for mobile menus, settings panels and command surfaces.

Category: Interaction
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/drawer
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/drawer.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/drawer`
Copy dependencies: `use-portal-root`

Import:
```tsx
import { Drawer, DrawerContent, DrawerTrigger } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Drawer>
  <DrawerTrigger>Open drawer</DrawerTrigger>
  <DrawerContent>
    <DrawerHeader>
      <DrawerTitle>Settings</DrawerTitle>
      <DrawerDescription>Panel content lives here.</DrawerDescription>
    </DrawerHeader>
    <DrawerClose>Close</DrawerClose>
  </DrawerContent>
</Drawer>
```

Composition:
```txt
Drawer
|-- DrawerTrigger
`-- DrawerContent
    |-- DrawerHeader
    |   |-- DrawerTitle
    |   `-- DrawerDescription
    `-- DrawerClose
```

Props:
- `open`: `boolean` - Controlled open state.
- `defaultOpen`: `boolean` default false - Initial uncontrolled open state.
- `DrawerContent.side`: `"left" | "right" | "top" | "bottom"` default "right" - Where the drawer enters from.
- `onOpenChange`: `(open: boolean) => void` - Called when the drawer opens or closes.

### DropdownMenu

A compact actions menu for navs, profile menus and table rows.

Category: Interaction
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/dropdown-menu
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/dropdown-menu.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/dropdown-menu`
Copy dependencies: `use-portal-root`

Import:
```tsx
import {
  DropdownMenu,
  DropdownMenuContent,
  DropdownMenuItem,
  DropdownMenuTrigger,
} from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<DropdownMenu>
  <DropdownMenuTrigger>Actions</DropdownMenuTrigger>
  <DropdownMenuContent>
    <DropdownMenuItem>Edit</DropdownMenuItem>
    <DropdownMenuItem>Duplicate</DropdownMenuItem>
  </DropdownMenuContent>
</DropdownMenu>
```

Composition:
```txt
DropdownMenu
|-- DropdownMenuTrigger
`-- DropdownMenuContent
    |-- DropdownMenuItem
    |-- DropdownMenuSeparator
    `-- DropdownMenuItem
```

Props:
- `DropdownMenuTrigger`: `ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>` - Button props forwarded to the trigger.
- `DropdownMenuContent.align`: `"start" | "end"` default "start" - Aligns the menu to the trigger.
- `DropdownMenuItem`: `ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>` - Button props forwarded to each item.

### Popover

A floating panel for filters, quick settings and small editable surfaces.

Category: Interaction
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/popover
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/popover.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/popover`
Copy dependencies: `use-portal-root`

Import:
```tsx
import { Popover, PopoverContent, PopoverTrigger } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Popover>
  <PopoverTrigger>Filters</PopoverTrigger>
  <PopoverContent>Quick controls live here.</PopoverContent>
</Popover>
```

Composition:
```txt
Popover
|-- PopoverTrigger
`-- PopoverContent
```

Props:
- `open`: `boolean` - Controlled open state.
- `defaultOpen`: `boolean` default false - Initial uncontrolled open state.
- `align`: `"start" | "end"` default "start" - Aligns the content to the trigger.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to root, trigger or content slots.

### Tabs

Tabbed sections for settings, dashboards and previews.

Category: Interaction
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/tabs
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/tabs.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/tabs`

Import:
```tsx
import { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Tabs defaultValue="preview">
  <TabsList>
    <TabsTrigger value="preview">Preview</TabsTrigger>
    <TabsTrigger value="code">Code</TabsTrigger>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="preview">Preview content</TabsContent>
  <TabsContent value="code">Code content</TabsContent>
</Tabs>
```

Composition:
```txt
Tabs
|-- TabsList
|   `-- TabsTrigger
`-- TabsContent
```

Props:
- `defaultValue`: `string` required - Initial active tab value.
- `value`: `string` - Controlled active tab value.
- `onValueChange`: `(value: string) => void` - Called when a tab is selected.
- `TabsTrigger.value`: `string` required - Value this trigger activates.

### Tooltip

A compact hover/focus label for icon buttons and dense UI.

Category: Interaction
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/tooltip
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/tooltip.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/tooltip`
Copy dependencies: `use-portal-root`

Import:
```tsx
import { Tooltip } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Tooltip content="Save changes">
  <Button>Save</Button>
</Tooltip>
```

Props:
- `content`: `ReactNode` required - Tooltip label content.
- `children`: `ReactNode` required - Element that owns the tooltip.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the tooltip wrapper.

## Layout

### AppShell

Composable application layout primitives for sidebars, navbars and bounded page content.

Category: Layout
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/app-shell
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/app-shell.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/app-shell`

Import:
```tsx
import {
  AppShell,
  AppShellBody,
  AppShellContent,
  AppShellMain,
  AppShellNavbar,
  AppShellSidebar,
} from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<AppShell>
  <AppShellSidebar aria-label="Workspace navigation">
    <nav>...</nav>
  </AppShellSidebar>
  <AppShellBody>
    <AppShellNavbar>Studio workspace</AppShellNavbar>
    <AppShellMain>
      <AppShellContent>
        Page content
      </AppShellContent>
    </AppShellMain>
  </AppShellBody>
</AppShell>
```

Composition:
```txt
AppShell
|-- AppShellSidebar
`-- AppShellBody
    |-- AppShellNavbar
    `-- AppShellMain
        `-- AppShellContent
```

Props:
- `AppShell.children`: `ReactNode` required - Composed shell regions, usually sidebar and body.
- `AppShell.size`: `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` default "md" - Controls shared spacing and navbar height.
- `AppShell.variant`: `"default" | "compact"` default "default" - Switches between regular and denser shell chrome.
- `AppShell.tone`: `"default" | "white" | "black"` default "default" - Applies the root shell surface treatment.
- `AppShell.asChild`: `boolean` default false - Renders the child as the root shell element.
- `AppShellSidebar.width`: `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` default "md" - Controls the responsive sidebar width.
- `AppShellSidebar.collapseBelow`: `"md" | "lg" | "never"` default "md" - Viewport where the sidebar is hidden below.
- `AppShellSidebar.side`: `"left" | "right"` default "left" - Places the sidebar on either side of the shell.
- `AppShellSidebar.sticky`: `boolean` default true - Pins the sidebar during page scrolling.
- `AppShellNavbar.sticky`: `boolean` default true - Pins the navbar to the top edge while scrolling.
- `AppShellContent.width`: `"sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" | "full"` default "lg" - Controls the maximum width of the content rail.

### Avatar

A framed user image or fallback for accounts, comments and navs.

Category: Layout
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/avatar
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/avatar.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/avatar`

Import:
```tsx
import { Avatar, AvatarFallback, AvatarImage } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Avatar>
  <AvatarFallback>RS</AvatarFallback>
</Avatar>
```

Props:
- `Avatar`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>` - Props forwarded to the root avatar.
- `AvatarImage`: `ImgHTMLAttributes<HTMLImageElement>` - Props forwarded to the image.
- `AvatarFallback`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>` - Props forwarded to the fallback.

### Breadcrumb

Navigation trail primitives for docs and app pages.

Category: Layout
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/breadcrumb
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/breadcrumb.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/breadcrumb`

Import:
```tsx
import { Breadcrumb, BreadcrumbItem, BreadcrumbLink, BreadcrumbList, BreadcrumbPage } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Breadcrumb>
  <BreadcrumbList>
    <BreadcrumbItem><BreadcrumbLink href="/">Docs</BreadcrumbLink></BreadcrumbItem>
    <BreadcrumbSeparator />
    <BreadcrumbItem><BreadcrumbPage>Components</BreadcrumbPage></BreadcrumbItem>
  </BreadcrumbList>
</Breadcrumb>
```

Props:
- `Breadcrumb`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>` - Props forwarded to the nav.
- `BreadcrumbLink`: `AnchorHTMLAttributes<HTMLAnchorElement> & { as?: ElementType }` - Props forwarded to links. Use as to render framework links like Next.js Link.
- `BreadcrumbPage`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>` - Current page item.

### Container

A responsive page-width wrapper for sections, shells and documentation layouts.

Category: Layout
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/container
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/container.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/container`

Import:
```tsx
import { Container } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Container size="md">
  <Card>
    <CardContent>
      <Title order={2} size="h4">Contained section</Title>
      <Text tone="muted" weight="bold">
        Keep page content aligned without rewriting max-width classes.
      </Text>
    </CardContent>
  </Card>
</Container>
```

Props:
- `children`: `ReactNode` required - Container content.
- `size`: `"sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl"` default "lg" - Applies the standard max-width.
- `variant`: `"default" | "fluid"` default "default" - Uses a constrained or full-width layout.
- `tone`: `"default"` default "default" - Reserved tone hook for Swirski data attributes.
- `asChild`: `boolean` default false - Renders the child as the root element.
- `...divProps`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>` - Forwarded to the rendered container.

### Grid

A reusable CSS grid primitive for page sections, card clusters and dense interface layouts.

Category: Layout
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/grid
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/grid.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/grid`

Import:
```tsx
import { Grid } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Grid columns={3} gap="md" className="md:grid-cols-3">
  <Card><CardContent>One</CardContent></Card>
  <Card><CardContent>Two</CardContent></Card>
  <Card><CardContent>Three</CardContent></Card>
</Grid>
```

Props:
- `children`: `ReactNode` required - Grid content.
- `as`: `ElementType` default "div" - Custom root element or component.
- `columns`: `1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 | 12` - Applies a static grid column count.
- `gap`: `"none" | "xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" | "2xl"` - Applies a standard gap size.
- `align`: `"start" | "center" | "end" | "stretch"` - Applies item alignment.
- `content`: `"start" | "center" | "end" | "between"` - Applies grid content alignment.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the root grid.
- `...elementProps`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>` - Forwarded to the root element.

### Navbar

Composable header primitives with a reusable hamburger menu for mobile navigation.

Category: Layout
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/navbar
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/navbar.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/navbar`
Copy dependencies: `dropdown-menu`, `use-portal-root`

Import:
```tsx
import {
  MobileMenu,
  MobileMenuContent,
  MobileMenuLink,
  MobileMenuNav,
  MobileMenuTrigger,
  Navbar,
  NavbarActions,
  NavbarBrand,
  NavbarDropdown,
  NavbarDropdownContent,
  NavbarDropdownLink,
  NavbarDropdownTrigger,
  NavbarLink,
  NavbarNav,
} from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Navbar>
  <NavbarBrand href="/">Swirski UI</NavbarBrand>
  <NavbarNav aria-label="Main navigation">
    <NavbarLink href="/components" active>Components</NavbarLink>
    <NavbarLink href="/hooks">Hooks</NavbarLink>
    <NavbarDropdown>
      <NavbarDropdownTrigger>Nerd Stuff</NavbarDropdownTrigger>
      <NavbarDropdownContent align="end">
        <NavbarDropdownLink href="/system">System</NavbarDropdownLink>
        <NavbarDropdownLink href="/cli">CLI</NavbarDropdownLink>
        <NavbarDropdownLink href="/build-component">Build a component</NavbarDropdownLink>
      </NavbarDropdownContent>
    </NavbarDropdown>
  </NavbarNav>
  <NavbarActions>
    <MobileMenu>
      <MobileMenuTrigger />
      <MobileMenuContent>
        <MobileMenuNav aria-label="Mobile navigation">
          <MobileMenuLink href="/components" active>Components</MobileMenuLink>
          <MobileMenuLink href="/hooks">Hooks</MobileMenuLink>
          <MobileMenuLink href="/cli">CLI</MobileMenuLink>
        </MobileMenuNav>
      </MobileMenuContent>
    </MobileMenu>
  </NavbarActions>
</Navbar>
```

Composition:
```txt
Navbar
|-- NavbarBrand
|-- NavbarNav
|   |-- NavbarLink
|   `-- NavbarDropdown
|       |-- NavbarDropdownTrigger
|       |-- NavbarDropdownContent
|       `-- NavbarDropdownLink
`-- NavbarActions
    `-- MobileMenu
        |-- MobileMenuTrigger
        `-- MobileMenuContent
            |-- MobileMenuHeader
            |   |-- MobileMenuTitle
            |   `-- MobileMenuClose
            `-- MobileMenuNav
                `-- MobileMenuLink
```

Props:
- `Navbar`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>` - Props forwarded to the root header.
- `NavbarBrand`: `AnchorHTMLAttributes<HTMLAnchorElement>` - Props forwarded to the brand link.
- `NavbarNav`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>` - Props forwarded to the desktop nav.
- `NavbarLink.active`: `boolean` default false - Marks a link as the current page.
- `NavbarDropdownTrigger.active`: `boolean` default false - Styles the dropdown trigger as active.
- `NavbarDropdownContent.align`: `"start" | "end"` default "start" - Aligns the dropdown panel to the trigger.
- `NavbarDropdownLink.active`: `boolean` default false - Marks a dropdown link as the current page.
- `MobileMenu.open`: `boolean` - Controlled mobile menu open state.
- `MobileMenu.defaultOpen`: `boolean` default false - Initial uncontrolled open state.
- `MobileMenuContent.side`: `"left" | "right"` default "right" - Side used for the menu panel.
- `MobileMenuLink.closeOnSelect`: `boolean` default true - Closes the menu after a link is selected.

### Pagination

Page controls for tables, lists and archives.

Category: Layout
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/pagination
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/pagination.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/pagination`

Import:
```tsx
import { Pagination } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Pagination page={2} total={5} />
```

Props:
- `page`: `number` required - Current page.
- `total`: `number` required - Total number of pages.
- `onPageChange`: `(page: number) => void` - Called when a page button is pressed.

### Separator

A small visual divider for sections, menus and dense layouts.

Category: Layout
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/separator
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/separator.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/separator`

Import:
```tsx
import { Separator } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Separator />
```

Props:
- `orientation`: `"horizontal" | "vertical"` default "horizontal" - Divider direction.
- `className`: `string` - Adds classes to the divider.

### Table

A bold base table for data lists before adding DataTable behavior.

Category: Layout
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/table
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/table.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/table`

Import:
```tsx
import { Table, TableBody, TableCell, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Table>
  <TableHead>
    <TableRow><TableHeader>Name</TableHeader><TableHeader>Status</TableHeader></TableRow>
  </TableHead>
  <TableBody>
    <TableRow><TableCell>Studio</TableCell><TableCell>Live</TableCell></TableRow>
  </TableBody>
</Table>
```

Composition:
```txt
Table
|-- TableHead
|   `-- TableRow
|       `-- TableHeader
`-- TableBody
    `-- TableRow
        `-- TableCell
```

Props:
- `Table`: `TableHTMLAttributes<HTMLTableElement>` - Props forwarded to the table element.
- `TableHeader`: `ThHTMLAttributes<HTMLTableCellElement>` - Props forwarded to header cells.
- `TableCell`: `TdHTMLAttributes<HTMLTableCellElement>` - Props forwarded to body cells.

## Media

### ImageFrame

A framed media primitive for screenshots, product shots, visual examples and editorial layouts.

Category: Media
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/image-frame
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/image-frame.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/image-frame`

Import:
```tsx
import { ImageFrame } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<ImageFrame caption="Studio proof" tone="paper">
  <img src="/preview.jpg" alt="Preview" />
</ImageFrame>
```

Props:
- `children`: `ReactNode` required - Media or visual content rendered inside the frame.
- `caption`: `ReactNode` - Optional caption pinned inside the frame.
- `size`: `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` default "md" - Sets the Swirski size data attribute.
- `tone`: `"paper" | "white" | "black"` default "paper" - Applies the frame background and text treatment.
- `variant`: `"default" | "plain"` default "default" - Toggles the border and offset shadow treatment.
- `asChild`: `boolean` default false - Renders the child as the root element.
- `...divProps`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>` - Forwarded to the rendered frame.

## Theming

### SwirskiProvider

Theme provider for Swirski tokens, color schemes and per-app brand overrides.

Category: Theming
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/swirski-provider
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/swirski-provider.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/theme`

Import:
```tsx
import { SwirskiProvider, createSwirskiTheme } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
const theme = createSwirskiTheme({
  colorBlue: "#0047FF",
  colorYellow: "#FFE45C",
  shadowMd: "8px 8px 0 var(--sw-color-shadow)",
});

<SwirskiProvider theme={theme}>
  <App />
</SwirskiProvider>
```

Props:
- `colorScheme`: `"light" | "dark"` default "light" - Selects the default light or dark token set.
- `theme`: `SwirskiTheme` - Overrides any Swirski token or adds custom CSS variables.
- `createSwirskiTheme`: `(theme: SwirskiTheme) => SwirskiTheme` - Small helper for typed theme objects.
- `useSwirskiTheme`: `() => SwirskiThemeTokens` - Reads the resolved theme inside React components.

## Typography

### SectionLabel

A chunky rotated heading label for editorial section markers, callouts and page breaks.

Category: Typography
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/section-label
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/section-label.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/section-label`

Import:
```tsx
import { SectionLabel } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<SectionLabel tone="yellow">Featured</SectionLabel>
```

Props:
- `children`: `ReactNode` required - Label text.
- `size`: `"sm" | "md" | "lg"` default "md" - Applies the label padding and heading scale.
- `tone`: `"yellow" | "white" | "blue" | "red"` default "yellow" - Applies the label color treatment.
- `variant`: `"default" | "flat"` default "default" - Toggles the offset shadow treatment.
- `asChild`: `boolean` default false - Renders the child as the root element.
- `...headingProps`: `HTMLAttributes<HTMLHeadingElement>` - Forwarded to the rendered heading.

### Typography

Heading and body text primitives with semantic controls, reusable scales and Swirski tone presets.

Category: Typography
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/components/typography
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/typography.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/components/typography`

Import:
```tsx
import { Text, Title } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
<Title order={1} size="display">Build expressive interfaces.</Title>
<Text size="xl" tone="muted" weight="bold">
  A small UI library for expressive, editorial web interfaces.
</Text>
```

Props:
- `Title.children`: `ReactNode` required - Heading content.
- `Title.order`: `1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6` default 1 - Chooses the semantic heading element.
- `Title.size`: `"display" | "h1" | "h2" | "h3" | "h4" | "h5" | "h6"` - Overrides the visual heading scale.
- `Title.tone`: `"default" | "muted" | "inverted"` default "default" - Applies the standard title color treatment.
- `Title.as`: `ElementType` - Custom heading element or component.
- `Title.asChild`: `boolean` default false - Renders the child as the root element.
- `Text.children`: `ReactNode` required - Text content.
- `Text.component`: `"p" | "span" | "div"` default "p" - Chooses the rendered text element.
- `Text.size`: `"xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" | "2xl"` default "md" - Applies the standard text scale.
- `Text.variant`: `"default" | "lead" | "caption"` default "default" - Applies the standard text role treatment.
- `Text.tone`: `"default" | "muted" | "subtle" | "inverted"` default "default" - Applies the standard text color treatment.
- `Text.weight`: `"regular" | "medium" | "bold" | "black"` default "regular" - Applies the text weight.
- `Text.as`: `ElementType` - Custom text element or component.
- `Text.asChild`: `boolean` default false - Renders the child as the root element.

## Hooks

### useClickOutside

Runs a callback when pointer events happen outside a referenced element.

Category: Hooks
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/hooks/use-click-outside
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/use-click-outside.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/hooks/use-click-outside`

Import:
```tsx
import { useClickOutside } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

useClickOutside(ref, () => setOpen(false), {
  enabled: open,
});
```

Props:
- `ref`: `RefObject<HTMLElement | null>` required - Element that owns the inside area.
- `handler`: `(event: Event) => void` required - Runs when the event target is outside the ref.
- `options.enabled`: `boolean` default true - Turns listeners on or off.
- `options.events`: `Array<"mousedown" | "pointerdown" | "touchstart">` default ["pointerdown"] - Events to listen for.

Returns:
- `return`: `void` - Registers document listeners in an effect and returns no value.

### useClipboard

Copies text to the clipboard and tracks copied/error state.

Category: Hooks
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/hooks/use-clipboard
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/use-clipboard.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/hooks/use-clipboard`

Import:
```tsx
import { useClipboard } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
const clipboard = useClipboard();

<Button onClick={() => clipboard.copy("pnpm add @swirski/ui")}>
  {clipboard.copied ? "Copied" : "Copy"}
</Button>
```

Props:
- `options.timeout`: `number` default 1600 - Copied-state reset delay in milliseconds.

Returns:
- `copied`: `boolean` - True immediately after a successful copy.
- `copy`: `(value: string) => Promise<void>` - Copies text to the clipboard.
- `error`: `Error | null` - Last copy error.
- `reset`: `() => void` - Clears copied and error state.

### useControllableState

A small helper for components that support both controlled and uncontrolled state.

Category: Hooks
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/hooks/use-controllable-state
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/use-controllable-state.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/hooks/use-controllable-state`

Import:
```tsx
import { useControllableState } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
const [value, setValue] = useControllableState({
  value: props.value,
  defaultValue: props.defaultValue ?? "",
  onChange: props.onValueChange,
});
```

Props:
- `value`: `T` - Controlled value.
- `defaultValue`: `T` required - Uncontrolled initial value.
- `onChange`: `(value: T) => void` - Called when the setter resolves a value.

Returns:
- `value`: `T` - Current controlled or uncontrolled value.
- `setValue`: `Dispatch<SetStateAction<T>>` - Updates internal state or calls onChange with the resolved value.

### useDisclosure

Boolean open/close/toggle state for dialogs, drawers, popovers and custom disclosure UI.

Category: Hooks
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/hooks/use-disclosure
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/use-disclosure.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/hooks/use-disclosure`
Copy dependencies: `use-controllable-state`

Import:
```tsx
import { useDisclosure } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
const [opened, handlers] = useDisclosure(false);

<Button onClick={handlers.toggle}>
  {opened ? "Close" : "Open"}
</Button>
```

Props:
- `defaultOpened`: `boolean` default false - Initial uncontrolled disclosure state.
- `options.value`: `boolean` - Controlled disclosure state.
- `options.onChange`: `(opened: boolean) => void` - Called whenever state changes.

Returns:
- `opened`: `boolean` - Current disclosure state.
- `handlers.open`: `() => void` - Sets the disclosure state to open.
- `handlers.close`: `() => void` - Sets the disclosure state to closed.
- `handlers.toggle`: `() => void` - Flips the current disclosure state.
- `handlers.set`: `(opened: boolean) => void` - Sets the disclosure state directly.

### useEscapeKey

Runs a callback when Escape is pressed.

Category: Hooks
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/hooks/use-escape-key
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/use-escape-key.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/hooks/use-escape-key`

Import:
```tsx
import { useEscapeKey } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
useEscapeKey(() => setOpen(false), {
  enabled: open,
});
```

Props:
- `handler`: `(event: KeyboardEvent) => void` required - Runs when Escape is pressed.
- `options.enabled`: `boolean` default true - Turns the key listener on or off.
- `options.target`: `Document | HTMLElement | Window | null` - Event target, defaults to document.

Returns:
- `return`: `void` - Registers a keydown listener in an effect and returns no value.

### useIsPathnameActive

Checks whether a pathname matches the current URL path, including nested routes.

Category: Hooks
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/hooks/use-is-pathname-active
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/use-is-pathname-active.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/hooks/use-is-pathname-active`

Import:
```tsx
import { useIsPathnameActive } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
const active = useIsPathnameActive(
  "/components",
  "/components/button",
);
```

Props:
- `pathname`: `string | URL` required - Target pathname or URL to match.
- `currentUrl`: `string | URL` required - Current route URL or pathname.
- `options.exact`: `boolean` default false - Require an exact pathname match.

Returns:
- `active`: `boolean` - True when currentUrl matches pathname or a nested child path.

### useLocalStorage

Persists React state in localStorage with serializer hooks.

Category: Hooks
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/hooks/use-local-storage
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/use-local-storage.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/hooks/use-local-storage`

Import:
```tsx
import { useLocalStorage } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
const [cursor, setCursor, clearCursor] = useLocalStorage(
  "swirski-cursor",
  "bolt",
);
```

Props:
- `key`: `string` required - localStorage key.
- `defaultValue`: `T` required - Fallback value.
- `options.serialize`: `(value: T) => string` - Custom serializer.
- `options.deserialize`: `(value: string) => T` - Custom parser.

Returns:
- `value`: `T` - Current state, initialized from defaultValue and then hydrated from localStorage.
- `setValue`: `Dispatch<SetStateAction<T>>` - Updates React state and persists the serialized value.
- `removeValue`: `() => void` - Removes the stored item and resets state to defaultValue.

### useMediaQuery

Tracks a CSS media query in React state.

Category: Hooks
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/hooks/use-media-query
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/use-media-query.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/hooks/use-media-query`

Import:
```tsx
import { useMediaQuery } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
const isDesktop = useMediaQuery("(min-width: 1024px)");
```

Props:
- `query`: `string` required - CSS media query to observe.
- `initialValue`: `boolean` default false - Initial value before the browser runs the query.

Returns:
- `matches`: `boolean` - Whether the media query currently matches.

### usePortalRoot

Returns the document body once the app has mounted so portal components can render safely.

Category: Hooks
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/hooks/use-portal-root
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/use-portal-root.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/hooks/use-portal-root`

Import:
```tsx
import { usePortalRoot } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
const portalRoot = usePortalRoot();
```

Returns:
- `portalRoot`: `HTMLElement` - Root element to use for portals.

### useReducedMotion

Reads the user preference for reduced motion.

Category: Hooks
Docs: https://ui.swirski.dev/hooks/use-reduced-motion
Registry: https://ui.swirski.dev/r/use-reduced-motion.json
Source: `packages/ui/src/hooks/use-reduced-motion`
Copy dependencies: `use-media-query`

Import:
```tsx
import { useReducedMotion } from "@swirski/ui";
```

Usage:
```tsx
const reducedMotion = useReducedMotion();
```

Props:
- `initialValue`: `boolean` default false - Initial value before the media query runs.

Returns:
- `reducedMotion`: `boolean` - True when prefers-reduced-motion is reduce.

## Workspace Development

```txt
apps/docs        Documentation site
packages/ui      React component package
packages/cli     Local registry CLI
registry         Component registry manifest
```

Common commands:

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm dev
pnpm storybook
pnpm docs:metadata
pnpm llms:build
pnpm build:ui
pnpm registry:shadcn
pnpm test:ui
pnpm build
```

## Current Quality Notes

Swirski UI has a broad component surface, docs pages, playgrounds, generated props metadata, examples, component tests for several interactive primitives, shared modal overlay behavior for Dialog and Drawer, shared Floating UI positioning for DropdownMenu, Popover, Select, and Tooltip, and hosted shadcn-compatible registry output.

Important near-term quality work:

- Validate hosted shadcn registry items in CI.
- Smoke test remote shadcn installs for key components.
- Add per-component install tabs for package, Swirski CLI, and shadcn workflows.
- Continue strengthening accessibility tests for complex interaction primitives.

Swirski UI is released under the MIT License.

License file: https://github.com/rutgerswirski/swirski-ui/blob/main/LICENSE
